key_points: A ratio compares two quantities and shows their relationship
Ratios can be simplified by dividing both terms by their common factor
Equivalent ratios represent the same proportional relationship
A proportion is formed when two ratios are equal
Proportional reasoning helps solve problems involving scaling and comparisons
examples: Real-life example: recipe ingredients scaled for more servings
formulas: Simplifying ratio → divide both numbers by HCF
Proportion rule → a:b = c:d if a × d = b × c
exam_tips: Convert quantities to the same units before forming ratios
Use HCF to simplify ratios quickly
Use cross multiplication to verify proportions
Check answers carefully in proportion problems
